question.label: Notes doc questions
question.name: What kinds of questions are sitting in that notes doc right now?
question.description: Turns the notes-doc habit into concrete unclear areas so the manager can remove friction fast.

answer: I would benefit from a clearer guide for what to escalate quickly versus what to batch.

assessment.note: Names a specific missing guide for escalation versus batching, which is a strong clarity gap and shows hesitation to engage directly.

new_queue.label: Escalate vs batch
new_queue.name: What would a useful escalate-versus-batch guide need to cover?

new_queue.label: Context gaps
new_queue.name: What context are you still waiting on before you change a design or make an architecture call?

new_queue.label: Incident norms
new_queue.name: How should you know when to escalate during an incident here?

new_queue.label: Support network
new_queue.name: Who do you want to lean on first when you hit something unclear?

new_queue.label: Useful next step
new_queue.name: What would make the next two weeks feel steadier for you, Riley?

issues: coverage: injected engagement into next question (0 touches after turn 5)

history.answer_excerpt: I would benefit from a clearer guide for what to escalate quickly versus what to batch.
I am comfortable reading the services, but I am less sure how decisions are really made here.
I can trace most request paths now, though I still miss historical context behind certain tradeoffs.
I have not been fully clear on who is on point for each domain when priorities conflict.
I am trying not to interrupt people, so sometimes I keep questions in a notes doc instead of asking.
I would benefit from a clearer guide for what to escalate quickly versus what to batch.
